rationale: The citing paper explicitly frames its contribution as extending prior object-specific scarcity/availability research, and the cited paper is a directly relevant scarcity study on perceived limited availability with a specific process account (attention to quantity) and downstream choice effects. With a DOI-verified reference match (10.1509/jmr.12.0090) and strong conceptual overlap in core constructs, this looks plausibly foundational/theory-relevant rather than an obvious incidental mention.
